name: David Nadien
abstract: David Nadien (March 12, 1926 – May 28, 2014) was an American virtuoso violinist and violin teacher. He was the concertmaster of the New York Philharmonic from 1966 to 1970.

concerts.label: New York Philharmonic Young People's Concert

works.t: Violin Concerto in E Minor, Op. 64: 1. Allegro molto appassionato
Violin Concerto in A Minor, Op. 82
Violin Concerto in D Major, Op. 61
Concerto Grosso in C Minor, Op. 6, No. 3

about_quotes.speaker: Rob Mounsey
about_quotes.evidence: David Nadien, the brilliant concertmaster for the Philharmonic ... was laughing at me a little bit. He knew that I knew better. He said, “Rob, Rob, we are not violas.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Rob Mounsey interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, April 9, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: Anne Phillips
about_quotes.evidence: the people that I had known doing demos ... David Nadien, who was the concertmaster for Bernstein. That happened while doing it
about_quotes.doc_title: Anne Phillips interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, February 24, 2025
